_doc: Audit canary manifest (R4). Each canary is a deliberately-broken fixture that the audit MUST flag every run. If any canary stops being flagged, the audit has regressed. Based on the compliance-testing 'test injection' pattern — see Reference/Research/2026-05-28_audit-upgrade-best-practices.md §4 Gap 4.
_iron_rule: These fixtures live at tests/audit_canaries/ and must NEVER be moved without updating this manifest. The audit's Phase 0 canary verification reads this file and confirms each expected_pattern appears in the corresponding flagged finding.

canaries.name: credential_pattern_canary
canaries.purpose: Verifies Phase 2.6 credential grep still detects sk-ant- pattern outside of .env.

canaries.name: stub_claudemd_canary
canaries.purpose: Verifies Phase 2 still flags placeholder/boilerplate CLAUDE.md content.
canaries.expected_pattern: <-- Describe what this workspace is for -->

canaries.name: orphan_binding_canary
canaries.purpose: Verifies Phase 2.8 still flags subagent bindings with no 'when to use' trigger.
canaries.expected_pattern: description: This binding has no clear activation trigger
